summary: Plan-only assessment: #89997 is a narrow, useful contributor PR and the canonical path for this shard, but it is not merge-ready because mergeability is unknown, checks were not hydrated due a GraphQL rate-limit error, review comments were not hydrated, and no Codex /review preflight is present. Recommend repairing/finalizing the contributor branch rather than replacing it.

actions.reason: Keep #89997 as the canonical contributor PR, but require a repair/finalization pass before merge: re-fetch live state, hydrate checks and review comments, rebase or refresh if needed, run Codex /review, run targeted validation, and preserve contributor credit.
Emit an executable repair artifact for Clownfish to finalize the contributor branch through guarded validation and review rather than opening an unrelated replacement PR first.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair and finalize contributor PR #89997 by refreshing live state, hydrating checks and review comments, rebasing or otherwise updating the maintainer-editable branch if needed, running Codex /review, and validating the narrow CLI startup-policy change before any merge recommendation.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(cli): suppress mcp serve startup stdout
repair_candidate.pr_body: Repairs and finalizes contributor PR #89997 by @kenners22. The change treats `openclaw mcp serve` as stdio protocol mode for startup policy purposes so stdout remains JSON-RPC-only at startup. Validation should include `git diff --check`, `pnpm -s vitest run src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts`, `pnpm check:changed`, and a clean Codex `/review`.
credit_notes: Preserve kenners22 as the source PR author and credit PR #89997 in any repair or replacement path.
If Clownfish must open a follow-up PR after repair fails, include source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89997 and attribution to @kenners22 in the PR body.
